# Aionis Runtime v0.3.0 Draft

Aionis Runtime is the self-evolving continuity execution-memory engine for agent systems.

This release turns the product into a cleaner public shape:

- a standalone local-first runtime package
- a stronger public SDK decision layer
- clearer startup gates for hosts and operators
- docs and quickstarts that no longer assume a source checkout as the primary install path

## Highlights

### Standalone runtime install path

You can now start the local runtime with:

```bash
npx @ostinato/aionis-runtime start
```

This makes first-run evaluation much lighter than cloning the full monorepo before you can even boot the runtime.

### Action Retrieval is now first-class

The public SDK and runtime now expose a dedicated action-retrieval layer instead of burying the next move inside a single compact kickoff summary.

That layer surfaces:

- selected tool
- recommended file path
- recommended next action
- evidence entries
- uncertainty and gate actions

### Uncertainty and operator gates are explicit

Weak retrieval is no longer flattened into a fake confident first action.

The runtime can now escalate into:

- `inspect_context`
- `widen_recall`
- `rehydrate_payload`
- `request_operator_review`

Hosts can consume these through operator projection and action hints instead of reverse-engineering planner output.

### Replay and runtime internals were tightened further

This release also continues the internal cleanup work:

- replay run flow is more modular
- startup/runtime posture stays loopback-safe by default
- release checks now include the standalone runtime package

## Recommended package release set

- `@ostinato/aionis-runtime@0.1.0`
- `@ostinato/aionis@0.3.0`

## Publish readiness note

- `@ostinato/aionis-runtime@0.1.0` dry-run publish is already clean
- `@ostinato/aionis@0.2.0` dry-run publish correctly fails because `0.2.0` is already published
- before the real SDK publish step, bump `@ostinato/aionis` from `0.2.0` to `0.3.0`

## Validation

- `npm run -s build`
- `npm run -s docs:check`
- `npm run -s packages:release:check`
- `npm run -s runtime:publish:dry-run`
- `npm run -s sdk:publish:dry-run`
- `npm run -s lite:test`

## Upgrade path

### First-time users

Start the runtime:

```bash
npx @ostinato/aionis-runtime start
```

Then install the SDK in your own project:

```bash
npm install @ostinato/aionis
```

### Existing repo users

The source-checkout path still works:

```bash
npm install
npm run lite:start
```

But it is no longer the recommended first-contact install path.
